matching: case-insensitive invariant substring over the RAW feedback text; patterns are Finnish stems on purpose so inflected forms match (e.g. 'loukkaantu' matches loukkaantui/loukkaantunut). Phase 2's deterministic alert layer implements exactly this contract — no tokenization, no lemmatizer, no model.
deliberateExclusions: 'sattu/sattui' — dominant Finnish sense is tapahtua ('ei sattunut pahemmin'); as a keyword it floods false positives. The injury sense belongs to the LLM layer.

structural-failure verbs (sortua, pettää, irrota, antaa periksi, romahtaa) — deliberately NOT keywords: they are exactly the no-keyword safety story's vocabulary, detectable only by understanding. Putting them here would make the demo's centerpiece trivially rule-detectable.
'reklamaatio' — routine retail vocabulary, not a legal threat.
'home/homehtunut' — a quality complaint whose severity assessment belongs to the LLM layer, not an instant alert.
